suggestions.message: Type 'unknown' must have a '[Symbol.iterator]()' method that returns an iterator.

issues.description: The PUT /:departmentId endpoint uses `string` type for departmentId without UUID format validation (unlike the GET and DELETE endpoints which use `string & tags.Format<'uuid'>`). This inconsistency could allow malformed IDs to reach the provider layer.
issues.suggestion: Change the departmentId parameter type to `string & tags.Format<'uuid'>` to match the other endpoints and ensure consistent input validation.

issues.description: The organizationId path parameter is typed as plain `string` without UUID format validation. Activity logs contain sensitive audit data. A malformed or crafted organizationId could potentially bypass organization isolation checks if the provider does not independently validate the format.
issues.suggestion: Type organizationId as `string & tags.Format<'uuid'>` to enforce UUID format at the framework level before reaching provider logic.

issues.description: Multiple timesheetId path parameters (at, update, erase, submit, reject endpoints) are typed as plain `string` without UUID format validation, while the approve endpoint correctly uses `string & tags.Format<'uuid'>`. Inconsistent validation means some endpoints may receive malformed IDs.
issues.suggestion: Standardize all timesheetId parameters to `string & tags.Format<'uuid'>` across all timesheet endpoints for consistent input validation.

issues.description: The index (PATCH), at (GET), update (PUT), and erase (DELETE) endpoints use plain `string` for projectId and taskId parameters without UUID format validation, while the create (POST) endpoint correctly validates projectId as `string & tags.Format<'uuid'>`. This inconsistency could allow malformed IDs to reach provider logic.
issues.suggestion: Apply `string & tags.Format<'uuid'>` to all projectId and taskId parameters across all task endpoints for consistent validation.

issues.description: All controllers use `console.log(error)` for error handling. This is the standard AutoBE pattern but lacks structured logging with request context (user ID, organization ID, request ID), making security incident investigation difficult.
issues.suggestion: Consider integrating a structured logging library (e.g., Winston, Pino) with contextual metadata for production deployments.

issues.description: No rate limiting is applied to any of these endpoints. Write operations (POST, PUT, DELETE) on organizational data could be abused.
issues.suggestion: Apply rate limiting at the API gateway or using NestJS ThrottlerModule, especially for write operations.

issues.description: No authorization check before reactivating a project. Any authenticated member can reactivate any archived project regardless of their role or permissions. Unlike putErpHrmMemberProjectsProjectId.ts which checks for 'project:manage' permission or project-lead role, this provider performs no such validation.
issues.suggestion: Add the same permission check as in putErpHrmMemberProjectsProjectId.ts: verify the caller is a member of the project's organization and has 'project:manage' permission or is a project-lead for the specific project.

issues.description: No authorization check before creating a contract. Any authenticated member can create a contract for any organization_member_id without verifying they have HR management permissions. The provider only checks that the target organization member exists, but does not verify the caller has permission to manage contracts.
issues.suggestion: Add a permission check to verify the caller has 'hr:manage' or equivalent permission within the same organization as the target organization member before allowing contract creation.

issues.description: No authorization check before rejecting a timesheet. Any authenticated member can reject any submitted timesheet. The provider does not verify the caller has manager/approver permissions ('time:manage' or similar) before allowing the rejection action.
issues.suggestion: Add a permission check to verify the caller has 'time:manage' or timesheet approval permissions within the same organization as the timesheet owner before allowing rejection.

issues.description: No authorization check before creating a role. Any authenticated member associated with an organization can create new roles without verifying they have 'org:manage' or 'role:manage' permissions. This allows privilege escalation by creating roles with elevated permissions.
issues.suggestion: Add a permission check similar to putErpHrmMemberRolesRoleId.ts: verify the caller has 'org:manage' or 'role:manage' permission before allowing role creation.

issues.description: Insufficient authorization check for updating organization member details. The provider only verifies the caller is an active member of the same organization (callerMembership check), but does not verify they have 'org:manage' or 'member:manage' permissions. Any organization member can update any other member's role, department, position, employment type, and active status.
issues.suggestion: Add a permission check to verify the caller has 'org:manage' or equivalent administrative permission before allowing updates to other members' details.

issues.description: No authorization check before modifying role permissions. Any authenticated member can modify permissions of any non-builtin role. The provider only checks if the role is built-in, but does not verify the caller has 'org:manage' or 'role:manage' permissions. This allows privilege escalation.
issues.suggestion: Add a permission check to verify the caller has 'org:manage' or 'role:manage' permission within the role's organization before allowing permission modifications.

issues.description: The profile update uses props.member.id directly from the JWT payload to scope the update, which is correct for self-service profile updates. However, there is no validation that fields like 'avatarUrl' contain safe URLs, potentially allowing storage of malicious URLs.
issues.suggestion: Validate avatarUrl against an allowlist of trusted domains or URL patterns if profile images are served back to users.

issues.description: The timer listing endpoint does not apply per-user filtering by default. While it scopes by organization, it does not restrict results to the authenticated member's own timers unless a 'time:view-all' permission check is implemented. The current code only filters by organization_id without checking if the user has permission to view all timers.
issues.suggestion: Add a permission check similar to patchErpHrmMemberTimelogs.ts: check for 'time:view-all' permission and restrict to own timers if the permission is absent.

issues.description: All controller methods use console.log(error) for error handling, which may expose sensitive stack traces and lacks structured logging.
issues.suggestion: Replace console.log with a structured logger (e.g., NestJS Logger) and ensure sensitive data is not logged. This is a standard AutoBE pattern but should be addressed before production deployment.

issues.description: No rate limiting is applied to timer endpoints. Endpoints like POST /timers (create) could be abused to create many timers rapidly.
issues.suggestion: Apply rate limiting at the infrastructure level (API gateway, reverse proxy) or use NestJS ThrottlerModule.

issues.description: No ownership validation when fetching a session by ID. Any authenticated guest can retrieve any session by guessing or knowing a valid session UUID, exposing other users' session data (IP, referrer, expiry, etc.).
issues.suggestion: After fetching the session, verify that session.erp_hrm_member_id matches the member ID associated with props.guest.session_id. Throw a 403 or 404 if the session does not belong to the requesting guest.

issues.description: When props.body.organizationMemberId is provided, the query uses it directly without verifying that the requesting member has permission to view another member's timesheets. This allows any authenticated member to enumerate timesheets of arbitrary organization members.
issues.suggestion: Validate that the requesting member either owns the organizationMemberId or has an administrative/manager role before allowing cross-member timesheet queries.

issues.description: Any authenticated member can add permissions to any non-built-in role without verifying that the requesting member has an administrative role or owns/manages the target role. This could allow privilege escalation.
issues.suggestion: Check that the requesting member has an admin or role-management permission before allowing permission assignment to roles.

issues.description: Any authenticated member can create a new organization without any role or permission check. Organization creation is typically an administrative action.
issues.suggestion: Verify that the requesting member has an appropriate administrative permission before allowing organization creation.

issues.description: No rate limiting is applied to session listing endpoints. Repeated queries could be used to enumerate session metadata.
issues.suggestion: Apply rate limiting at the infrastructure or middleware level for all API endpoints.

issues.description: Privileged operations such as adding permissions to roles are not audit-logged, making it difficult to detect unauthorized privilege escalation after the fact.
issues.suggestion: Add structured audit logging for all role and permission modification operations, recording who performed the action, when, and what changed.

agentEvaluations.summary: [sampled 30/110 files] [3 chunks, 23→22 issues] The codebase uses MemberAuth decorators consistently across all endpoints, providing a baseline authentication layer. The main concerns are inconsistent UUID format validation on path parameters — some endpoints correctly use `string & tags.Format<'uuid'>` while others use plain `string`, creating an inconsistent security boundary before provider logic. The activity log controller handles sensitive audit data with unvalidated string organizationId. No critical injection or cryptographic issues are present in the controller layer. Standard AutoBE patterns (console.log, no rate limiting) are present but flagged at suggestion level only.

issues.description: Missing permission check: Anyone who belongs to the organization can update any member's role/department. According to business rules, role/department assignment should be restricted to managers or HR roles.
issues.suggestion: Add permission check to verify caller has 'org:manage' or 'employee:manage' permission.

issues.description: Missing permission check: Anyone can update project member role without verifying caller has project management permissions.
issues.suggestion: Add authorization check: caller must be project-lead or have 'project:manage' permission.

issues.description: Missing ownership/permission check: Anyone can update any contract without verifying they are authorized.
issues.suggestion: Add check that caller is either the contract owner's manager, HR, or has contract management permission.

issues.description: Logic error: timesheet status validation incorrectly checks 'draft' status only, but according to requirements, 'rejected' timesheets can also be updated (since they go back to draft).
issues.suggestion: Change status check to allow updates on 'draft' OR 'rejected' status.

issues.description: Missing validation: timelogsToAdd may belong to different organization member (should belong to same member as timesheet).
issues.suggestion: Add check to ensure timelogsToAdd belong to the same organization_member_id as the timesheet.

issues.description: Unrealistic assumption: timelog count validation assumes all timelogs are valid (not deleted).
issues.suggestion: Add deleted_at null condition to timelog count query.

issues.description: Unrealistic assumption: duration calculation assumes start_time/end_time are strings, but database stores datetime.
issues.suggestion: Ensure start_time/end_time are converted correctly; possibly use Prisma's date handling.

issues.description: Logic error: reviewer should be a manager/higher role, not just any member.
issues.suggestion: Add permission check: reviewer must have 'timesheet:review' permission.

issues.description: Multiple files import unused dependencies (IErpHrmDepartment, IErpHrmMember, etc.) that are not used in the function.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up code.

issues.description: Multiple hallucinated imports across files: '@prisma/sdk' does not exist. Prisma client is typically imported from '@prisma/client', not '@prisma/sdk'. This appears in all provider files.
issues.suggestion: Replace all instances of `import { Prisma } from "@prisma/sdk";` with `import { Prisma } from "@prisma/client";`

issues.description: Logic error: Line 30 queries for timer where `organization_member_id: props.member.id`. The `props.member.id` is a User ID, but the `organization_member_id` field expects an OrganizationMember ID. This will never find a timer.
issues.suggestion: First find the user's OrganizationMember record, then query timers using that ID: `organization_member_id: organizationMember.id`

issues.description: Logic error: The transaction creates a timelog using `ErpHrmTimerAtStopTransformer.select()`, but the transformer is likely for timers, not timelogs. The return type is `IErpHrmTimer.IStop`, but the function returns a transformed timelog.
issues.suggestion: Use a timelog transformer for the created timelog and ensure the return type matches the transformed data.

issues.description: Incomplete implementation: Task history creation uses `changedByMember: { connect: { id: props.member.id } }`. The `changedByMember` field likely expects an OrganizationMember ID, not a User ID.
issues.suggestion: Find the user's OrganizationMember ID within the project's organization and use that for the connection.

issues.description: Placeholder/hardcoded value: Line 20 casts `new Date().toISOString()` to `Date` type. This is incorrect type coercion and will likely cause runtime errors.
issues.suggestion: Use `new Date()` directly without `toISOString()` conversion.

issues.description: Unrealistic assumption: Collector is called with `erpHrmMemberSessions: { id: props.member.session_id }`, but `MemberPayload` may not have a `session_id` field, and the collector may not need it.
issues.suggestion: Review the `ErpHrmOrganizationCollector.collect` method signature and ensure the passed parameters are correct.

issues.description: Unrealistic assumption: The `satisfies Prisma.erp_hrm_organization_membersFindFirstArgs` clause is unnecessary and may cause type errors if the Prisma import is incorrect.
issues.suggestion: Remove the `satisfies` clause as it's not needed for runtime.

issues.description: Dead/unused imports across multiple files: `IEntity`, `IErpHrmDepartment`, `IErpHrmMember`, `IErpHrmOrganizationMember`, `IErpHrmProject`, `IErpHrmRole`, `IErpHrmTask`, `IErpHrmTimelog`, `ArrayUtil`, `jwt`, `typia`, `v4`, `PasswordUtil`, `toISOStringSafe` are imported but not used in many provider files.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up code.

issues.description: Inconsistent naming: The variable `organizationMember` is used, but the Prisma model is `erp_hrm_organization_members`. This is fine, but note the pattern.
issues.suggestion: No action needed, just noting the convention.

issues.description: Importing from '@prisma/sdk' is incorrect. The correct Prisma client import should be '@prisma/client'.
issues.suggestion: Change import { Prisma } from '@prisma/sdk'; to import { Prisma } from '@prisma/client';

issues.description: The function signature expects 'props.ip' but the code uses 'props.body.ip' (line 80) and 'props.ip' (line 80) inconsistently. The 'ip' parameter is passed in props object, not in body.
issues.suggestion: Use 'props.ip' consistently for IP address, not 'props.body.ip'.

issues.description: Missing email verification logic - token is created but not sent via email. The verification flow is incomplete.
issues.suggestion: Add email sending logic or integrate with an email service to send verification token to user.

issues.description: Function creates guest session with 'expired_at: accessExpires' (1 hour) but refresh token lasts 7 days. This mismatch could cause session validation issues.
issues.suggestion: Set session 'expired_at' to match refresh token expiration (7 days) for consistency.

issues.description: Function name 'patchErpHrmMemberTimesheets' suggests updating timesheets, but it implements a GET/list operation with filtering and pagination. This is a REST API design mismatch.
issues.suggestion: Rename function to 'getErpHrmMemberTimesheets' or change implementation to actually update timesheets as the name implies.

issues.description: Function name 'patchErpHrmMemberTimelogs' suggests updating timelogs, but it implements a GET/list operation. REST API design mismatch.
issues.suggestion: Rename function to 'getErpHrmMemberTimelogs' or implement actual PATCH functionality.

issues.description: Function updates a single permission but the implementation fetches multiple permissions and only updates the first one. This could lead to data inconsistency if multiple permissions exist.
issues.suggestion: Either enforce single permission per role or implement proper update logic for all matching permissions.

issues.description: Function tries to access 'props.member.organization_id' via type assertion, but MemberPayload type likely doesn't have this property. This will cause runtime errors.
issues.suggestion: Fetch the member's organization membership from database instead of assuming the property exists.

issues.description: Function name 'patchErpHrmMemberProjectsProjectId' suggests general update, but it only reactivates archived projects. Functionality is too specific for a generic PATCH endpoint.
issues.suggestion: Rename to something like 'reactivateErpHrmMemberProjectsProjectId' or implement full PATCH functionality.

issues.description: Unused imports: IEntity, ArrayUtil, typia. These imports are not used in the code.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up the code.

issues.description: Unused imports: IErpHrmDepartment, IErpHrmMember, IErpHrmOrganizationMember, IErpHrmRole, jwt, typia, v4, PasswordUtil, toISOStringSafe. These imports are not used in the code.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up the code.

issues.description: Multiple files import '@prisma/sdk' which doesn't exist. The correct import is '@prisma/client'.
issues.suggestion: Change all imports from '@prisma/sdk' to '@prisma/client' in all affected files.

issues.description: Multiple files import unused dependencies: jwt, typia, tags, v4, PasswordUtil, toISOStringSafe, IEntity, IErpHrmMember, IErpHrmDepartment, IErpHrmRole, etc.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up the code and improve maintainability.

issues.description: Function name 'patchErpHrmMemberProjects' suggests a PATCH operation (update), but the function implements a GET/list operation with filtering and pagination.
issues.suggestion: Rename function to 'getErpHrmMemberProjects' or implement actual PATCH logic for updating multiple projects.

issues.description: Function 'patchErpHrmMemberMembers' incorrectly uses IErpHrmOrganizationMember.IRequest instead of IErpHrmMember.IRequest based on its name and purpose.
issues.suggestion: Use correct interface IErpHrmMember.IRequest or rename function to 'patchErpHrmMemberOrganizationMembers'.

issues.description: Cursor pagination assumes cursor is base64-encoded JSON with created_at and id fields, but this is not documented or standardized across other pagination functions.
issues.suggestion: Standardize cursor pagination approach across all list functions or document the cursor format.

issues.description: Different pagination implementations across files: some use cursor-based, some use offset-based, some have both. Inconsistent handling of 'unassigned' department filter.
issues.suggestion: Standardize pagination and filtering patterns across all list functions.

issues.description: Function 'patchErpHrmGuestSessions' queries member sessions but is named for guest operations. Guest shouldn't have access to member sessions.
issues.suggestion: Review the actor permissions - guest should not access member sessions. Rename or restrict access appropriately.

issues.description: Importing from non-existent package '@prisma/sdk'. The correct package is '@prisma/client'.
issues.suggestion: Change import { Prisma } from '@prisma/sdk'; to import { Prisma } from '@prisma/client';

issues.description: Unused imports: IEntity, IErpHrmDepartment, IErpHrmMember, IErpHrmOrganizationMember, IErpHrmProject, IErpHrmRole, ArrayUtil, Prisma, jwt, typia, v4, PasswordUtil, toISOStringSafe.
issues.suggestion: Remove all unused imports to clean up the code.

issues.description: No description

agentEvaluations.summary: [5 chunks, 111→39 issues] The code has correct structure and follows conventions, but shows several authorization gaps, incomplete permission checks, and minor logic errors. Most functions handle basic validation but miss edge cases and proper role-based access control.

issues.description: Controller has routes for guest sessions that query member sessions tables (erp_hrm_member_sessions). The spec shows no guest sessions model - only member sessions exist. The controller references IErpHrmMemberSession and queries erp_hrm_member_sessions, but guests should not have access to member sessions.
issues.suggestion: Remove this controller or implement proper guest sessions based on spec models (erp_hrm_guests and related session tables if they exist).

issues.description: Multiple endpoints reference 'totalHours' field which is not defined in the erp_hrm_timesheets Prisma schema. The schema has duration_minutes in timelogs but no totalHours field in timesheets.
issues.suggestion: Remove references to totalHours field or calculate it dynamically from associated timelogs without storing it.

issues.description: Timesheet approval/reject endpoints return IErpHrmTimesheet but spec indicates these should update status fields (status, reviewed_by_id, reviewed_at, rejection_reason) which exist in schema. However, the controller references WebSocket events and additional business logic not in spec.
issues.suggestion: Ensure implementation only updates fields defined in schema without adding unspecified business logic.

issues.description: Timer stop endpoint returns IErpHrmTimer.IStop but spec shows timer model doesn't have a stop DTO. The operation should create a timelog (erp_hrm_timelogs) as per spec description.
issues.suggestion: Return created timelog entity instead of timer stop DTO.

issues.description: Timesheet delete endpoint mentions 'soft delete' but spec doesn't explicitly define soft delete behavior for timesheets (though deleted_at field exists). The implementation adds business logic about unlinking timelogs which may be beyond spec.
issues.suggestion: Implement simple soft delete (set deleted_at) without additional business logic unless specified.

issues.description: Controller defines PATCH /erpHrm/member/timelogs for listing/searching timelogs, but PATCH is not a standard HTTP method for read operations. The spec likely expects GET with query parameters or POST for search. This is a spec mismatch - using PATCH for index/search is unconventional and may not match the OpenAPI specification.
issues.suggestion: Change to GET with query parameters or POST if the spec defines a search endpoint. PATCH should be used for partial updates, not for listing/searching.

issues.description: Controller path '/erpHrm/member/timelogs' may not match the expected OpenAPI path structure. The spec likely defines paths under a different prefix or structure. This could be a minor path variation but should be verified against the actual OpenAPI spec.
issues.suggestion: Verify the controller path matches the OpenAPI specification exactly. If the spec uses a different prefix (e.g., '/api/v1/member/timelogs'), update the controller decorator accordingly.

issues.description: Controller defines route '/erpHrm/member/projects' but no corresponding OpenAPI specification path is provided in the ground truth. All endpoints in this controller (POST, PATCH, GET, PUT, DELETE, and custom PATCH archive/complete) are phantom without spec verification.
issues.suggestion: Remove this controller or verify against the full OpenAPI spec to ensure these endpoints exist.

issues.description: Controller defines route '/erpHrm/member/profile' with PUT operation but no corresponding OpenAPI specification path is provided in the ground truth.
issues.suggestion: Remove this controller or verify against the full OpenAPI spec to ensure this endpoint exists.

issues.description: Controller uses PATCH method for search/index operation at root path '/erpHrm/member/projects'. While this could be a valid REST pattern, it's unconventional and may indicate a mismatch with typical OpenAPI CRUD specifications which would use GET for listing/searching.
issues.suggestion: Consider aligning with REST conventions (GET for search) or verify the spec explicitly defines PATCH for search.

issues.description: Controller uses @TypedRoute.Patch() for index/search endpoint, but spec likely expects GET with query parameters. Using PATCH for GET operations is unconventional.
issues.suggestion: If spec defines GET endpoint with query parameters, change to GET method. If spec defines POST/PATCH for search, it's acceptable.

issues.description: Delete endpoint specification mentions checking for pending timesheets and active contracts before deletion, but Prisma schema shows no direct relation from organizations to timesheets/contracts - those belong to organization_members. This logic may be fabricated.
issues.suggestion: Verify if spec actually requires checking timesheets/contracts - if not, remove these checks or adjust to check through organization_members relation.

issues.description: Controller defines routes for role permissions management (POST, PATCH, GET, DELETE) but the provided Prisma schema does not include an 'erp_hrm_role_permissions' model. The schema only shows 'erp_hrm_roles' with a 'rolePermissions' relation, but the target model is not defined in the provided ground truth. This suggests the implementation is based on a non-existent model.
issues.suggestion: Remove this controller or ensure the 'erp_hrm_role_permissions' model exists in the Prisma schema. If permissions are managed differently, adjust the implementation accordingly.

issues.description: Controller references an 'avatar' field on the 'erp_hrm_members' model for upload and deletion. The provided Prisma schema chunks (01-07) do not show an 'avatar' field in any model, including 'erp_hrm_members' (which is not shown in the provided chunks but is implied). This field is fabricated.
issues.suggestion: Remove the avatar endpoints or ensure the 'avatar' field is defined in the 'erp_hrm_members' model schema.

issues.description: The 'index' method uses PATCH HTTP method for listing/searching tasks. Typically, listing/search operations use GET or POST. Using PATCH for a read operation is a spec mismatch, though not a hallucination of non-existent functionality.
issues.suggestion: Consider using GET or POST for search/list operations as per REST conventions. However, this may be an implementation detail.

issues.description: The 'index' method uses PATCH HTTP method for listing/searching project members. Similar to tasks, using PATCH for a read operation is a spec mismatch.
issues.suggestion: Consider using GET or POST for search/list operations as per REST conventions. However, this may be an implementation detail.

issues.description: Controller defines POST /erpHrm/member/organizations/:organizationId/logo and DELETE /erpHrm/member/organizations/:organizationId/logo endpoints, but there is no corresponding OpenAPI specification for organization logo operations in the provided ground truth. The Prisma schema for erp_hrm_organizations (not shown in this chunk) would need to have a logo_url field to support this, but it's not present in the provided spec chunks.
issues.suggestion: Remove these endpoints unless the OpenAPI spec explicitly defines organization logo upload/delete operations and the Prisma schema includes a logo_url field.

issues.description: The logo controller and its providers reference a logo_url field on the organization entity, but the provided Prisma schema chunks do not show any logo-related fields in the erp_hrm_organizations model. This suggests the code is using a field that doesn't exist in the database schema.
issues.suggestion: Remove logo-related functionality or ensure the Prisma schema includes the appropriate logo_url field.

issues.description: Controller uses PATCH method for listing/filtering activity logs (index endpoint), which is unconventional for read operations. Typically, GET with query parameters or POST for complex filtering would be used. The spec doesn't explicitly define the HTTP method, but PATCH for listing is semantically incorrect.
issues.suggestion: Consider using GET with query parameters or POST for complex filtering instead of PATCH for listing operations.

issues.description: The provider implements complex timelog association/dissociation logic (timelogsToAdd, timelogsToRemove) that may not be explicitly defined in the OpenAPI specification. While this could be part of a timesheet update operation, it adds business logic that might not be specified.
issues.suggestion: Ensure this logic aligns with the OpenAPI specification for timesheet updates.

issues.description: The provider includes complex permission checks (time:manage permission) and timesheet lock validation that may be implementation details beyond the basic spec. While reasonable for security, it's not explicitly defined in the provided ground truth.
issues.suggestion: This is acceptable as implementation detail, but ensure it doesn't contradict any authorization requirements in the spec.

issues.description: The code references 'role.rolePermissions' on the erp_hrm_organization_members model, but the Prisma schema shows no such relation. The erp_hrm_organization_members model has a relation to erp_hrm_roles via 'role', but erp_hrm_roles does not have a direct 'rolePermissions' field in the provided schema. The correct relation path would be 'role.rolePermissions' if it existed, but the schema shows erp_hrm_roles has 'rolePermissions' as a HAS relation. However, the query is selecting through a nested relation that may not be properly defined in the schema.
issues.suggestion: Check the Prisma schema for the correct relation path between erp_hrm_organization_members and erp_hrm_role_permissions. The query should likely join through the role relation, but the field name might be different.

issues.description: The code queries erp_hrm_organization_members with 'deleted_at: null' but the Prisma schema for erp_hrm_organization_members includes a 'deleted_at' field, so this is correct. However, the update logic doesn't handle soft deletion properly.
issues.suggestion: Ensure soft deletion logic is consistent with the schema definition.

issues.description: The code checks for permission 'time:approve' but the Prisma schema for erp_hrm_organization_members includes a 'deleted_at' field, so this is correct. However, the update logic doesn't handle soft deletion properly.
issues.suggestion: Ensure soft deletion logic is consistent with the schema definition.

issues.description: The code creates an initial task history entry with previous_status as empty string '', but the Prisma schema for erp_hrm_task_histories requires 'previous_status' and 'new_status' fields. Using empty string for previous_status when a task is created is invented logic not specified in the schema.
issues.suggestion: Consider if this is appropriate business logic or if it should be handled differently according to the specification.

issues.description: Function name 'patchErpHrmMemberTimesheets' suggests a PATCH endpoint, but the implementation is a paginated search/query operation (GET-like). This is a spec mismatch - PATCH should update resources, not return paginated lists.
issues.suggestion: This should likely be a GET endpoint for searching timesheets, not a PATCH. If the spec defines PATCH for updating, implement update logic instead of search.

issues.description: The code references 'erpHrmOrganizations: { id: organizationMember.organization_id }' but the Prisma schema shows the relation field is named 'organization' (singular), not 'erpHrmOrganizations'.
issues.suggestion: Change to 'organization: { id: organizationMember.organization_id }' to match the Prisma schema relation name.

issues.description: The code references 'erpHrmMemberSessions: { id: props.member.session_id }' but there's no direct relation from erp_hrm_organizations to erp_hrm_member_sessions in the provided schema. The session_id likely comes from the member payload, not a relation.
issues.suggestion: Remove the erpHrmMemberSessions reference unless there's a direct relation defined in the full schema.

issues.description: The code uses 'guest: { connect: { id: guestId } }' but the Prisma schema relation field for guest sessions is likely named differently (not 'guest'). The schema chunk doesn't show the guest sessions model.
issues.suggestion: Verify the correct relation field name from the full Prisma schema for erp_hrm_guest_sessions.

issues.description: Function 'patchErpHrmMemberRoles' references a route '/member/roles' that has no basis in the provided Prisma schema. The schema defines roles but doesn't specify any endpoint for listing or managing them.
issues.suggestion: Remove this endpoint unless it exists in the OpenAPI specification.

issues.description: Function 'patchErpHrmMemberProjectsProjectIdComplete' references a route '/member/projects/{projectId}/complete' that has no basis in the provided Prisma schema. The schema defines projects with status transitions but doesn't specify an endpoint for completing a project.
issues.suggestion: Remove this endpoint unless it exists in the OpenAPI specification.

issues.possibleAllegation: Function 'patchErpHrmMemberDepartments' references a route '/member/departments' that has no basis in the provided Prisma schema. The schema defines departments but doesn't specify endpoints for listing them.
issues.suggestion: Remove this endpoint unless it exists in the OpenAPI specification.
issues.description: No description

The function 'patchErpHrmMemberContracts' implements a PATCH endpoint for contracts, but there is no corresponding PATCH operation for contracts in the provided OpenAPI specification. The spec only defines CRUD operations for contracts, and this appears to be a fabricated paginated search endpoint.
issues.suggestion: Remove this endpoint unless a PATCH operation for contracts is defined in the OpenAPI spec.

issues.description: The authorization check references a permission 'TIMESHEET_VIEW_ALL' which is not defined in the Prisma schema. The schema defines role permissions but the specific permission string is not specified in the ground truth.
issues.suggestion: Verify the correct permission name from the spec or use a more generic authorization check.

issues.description: Permission check uses 'permission:project:manage' which does not exist in the Prisma schema. The schema has 'project:manage' permission in erp_hrm_role_permissions model.
issues.suggestion: Change permission check to 'project:manage' instead of 'permission:project:manage'

issues.description: Query selects 'role.name' from erp_hrm_organization_members, but the relation is to erp_hrm_roles which has 'name' field, not directly on organization_members. However, the bigger issue is using role name for permission check instead of checking role_permissions table.
issues.suggestion: Use proper permission check through erp_hrm_role_permissions table instead of checking role names

issues.description: Hard delete of project with cascade handling contradicts Prisma schema which shows soft delete pattern (deleted_at field). The spec shows projects have deleted_at field for soft deletion.
issues.suggestion: Use soft delete (set deleted_at) instead of hard delete to maintain consistency with schema

issues.description: Hard delete of timers (deleteMany) contradicts Prisma schema which doesn't show deleted_at for timers, but standard practice would be to stop/complete timers rather than delete them.
issues.suggestion: Consider completing timers or handling them differently rather than hard deletion

issues.description: Activity log action 'employee_deleted' is not a standard action type defined in the spec. The spec mentions actions like 'create', 'update', 'delete', 'login', 'logout'.
issues.suggestion: Use 'delete' as the action type instead of 'employee_deleted'

issues.description: Checking for 'pending' timesheet status, but the spec defines timesheet statuses as 'draft', 'submitted', 'approved', 'rejected'. No 'pending' status exists.
issues.suggestion: Check for 'submitted' status instead of 'pending'

agentEvaluations.summary: [11 chunks, 70→40 issues] Moderate hallucination level. Critical issues include phantom guest sessions controller and fabricated totalHours field. Most endpoints map correctly to spec models but some add unspecified business logic. Auth controllers appear faithful to implied authentication flows.
